title: Torrubia laxiflora

There is no information on the plant Torrubia laxiflora. However, I found information on Torrubia laxa (also known as Turbina or Torrubia) and  Torrubia nobilis (also known as Pueraria). 

However, if you are referring to Pueraria, here's a paragraph about Pueraria:

Pueraria, specifically Pueraria montana var. lobata, is a plant in the legume family that is native to East Asia. The health benefits of Pueraria include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve glucose metabolism, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pueraria include isoflavones, such as daidzein and genistein, as well as other compounds like puerarin and kakkontoide. Pueraria is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and extracts, which are often marketed as supplements for cardiovascular health and weight management.
